mechanism_narrative: Elevated federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly suppressing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns exceed 60% when FEDFUNDS > 4.0%.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending economic contraction, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in > 55% of triggered instances, or t-statistic for return difference is statistically insignificant at 95% confidence.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous deceleration in real GDP growth and acceleration in consumer prices creates a stagflationary environment that compresses corporate margins and equity valuations over the next month.
failure_condition: Joint signal produces positive returns in > 60% of cases, or interaction term coefficient is statistically indistinguishable from zero in OLS regression.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding industrial production reflects robust manufacturing activity and supply-side capacity utilization, generating positive short-term momentum in S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no statistical correlation with INDPRO levels, or positive return frequency drops below 50% when INDPRO > 100.

mechanism_narrative: Unsustainably high retail auto sales indicate peak consumer demand and inventory drawdown, forecasting a subsequent demand contraction and negative equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Returns remain positive in > 65% of triggered months, or RSAFS levels fail to predict subsequent sales declines in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirm broad-based labor market strength, supporting corporate revenue growth and positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Interaction effect yields negative returns in > 55% of cases, or joint signal loses predictive power when FEDFUNDS > 5.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Persistently elevated consumer price inflation forces central banks to maintain restrictive policy stances, increasing discount rates and suppressing S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Positive returns exceed 60% during tightening periods when CPIAUCSL > 315, or inflation loses predictive significance after controlling for rate expectations.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp acceleration in housing starts signals renewed construction activity and consumer confidence, triggering positive short-term equity market momentum over the next week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in > 55% of triggered instances, or housing starts fail to lead broader economic indicators in Granger causality tests.
